evidence: All 8 tools use consistent snake_case with 'openlex__' namespace prefix: openlex__zhlaw_search_laws, openlex__zhlaw_get_law, openlex__zhlaw_get_article, openlex__zhlaw_list_laws, openlex__zhlaw_find_education_laws, openlex__zhlaw_search_articles, openlex__zhlaw_get_law_metadata, openlex__zhlaw_update_cache
No spaces, dots, brackets, or special chars in any tool name
snake_case is an accepted convention per check criteria
Tool descriptions are multi-sentence with context (230-390 chars each)
ARCH-001.notes: Convention is snake_case (not camelCase which is 'preferred' but check says kebab/snake is 'acceptable'). All descriptions provide use-case context.

evidence: All tool docstrings are 230-386 chars (well above 50-char minimum, median ~300 chars)

Descriptions include use-case context and examples: e.g. 'Durchsucht Titel, Abkürzungen und Volltexte aller ~970 kantonalen Gesetze. Unterstützt FTS5-Syntax: AND, OR, NOT, Phrasensuche. Ergebnisse nach Relevanz sortiert (BM25-Algorithmus).'
Descriptions include known abbreviations, filter prefixes, synergy info
gaps: No XML-style <use_case>, <important_notes>, <example> tags in tool descriptions (check requires in at least 80% of tools)
No explicit <example> blocks with sample inputs
No 'NOT for...' negative disambiguation between similar tools
ARCH-002.notes: Descriptions are rich enough to be useful but lack the structured XML-tag pattern the check specifically tests for.

evidence: All not-found cases return structured responses with count=0 plus actionable message guidance
zhlaw_find_education_laws has a fallback: searches broader corpus and returns results with 'Keine Treffer im Bildungsrecht (412.x)...' message
zhlaw_search_laws not-found: 'Andere Suchbegriffe/Synonyme versuchen, Filter entfernen...oder FTS5-Syntax verwenden'
All messages include what to try next (alternative tool names, parameter adjustments)
Tested in test_server.py: test_not_found_is_a_normal_result_not_an_error, test_search_laws_no_hits_gives_tips
ARCH-003.notes: Not-found returns are normal results with guidance, never isError.

evidence: Settings class (pydantic_settings BaseSettings) in server.py: mcp_host, mcp_port, mcp_transport, mcp_cors_origins, log_level all from env vars
Tool handlers use ctx: Context parameter (transport-agnostic), no request.headers access
Transport selection via MCP_TRANSPORT env var or --http CLI flag
Dual transport: stdio (default) and streamable-http
Lifespan pattern with AppContext dataclass for shared resources
Tests verify: test_settings_defaults, test_settings_reads_env_vars
ARCH-004.notes: Full IoC: no hardcoded transport, all config via settings object, lifespan-scoped shared HTTP client.

evidence: No API keys, passwords, tokens, or connection strings found in source code
Server holds no secrets - all data sources are public (confirmed in docs/secret-management.md)
Settings class uses pydantic_settings; only non-secret env vars (MCP_HOST, MCP_PORT, MCP_CORS_ORIGINS, LOG_LEVEL)
No hardcoded values matching secret patterns found in src/
gaps: No .env.example file (but acceptable since there are literally no secrets to demonstrate)
No CI secret scanning workflow (gitleaks/trufflehog)
ARCH-005.notes: Server is intentionally a Stufe 1 no-secrets server. Gaps are minor given context.

evidence: 8 tools total - well within the recommended <= 12 threshold
No 1:1 API mapping: tools aggregate internally (e.g. zhlaw_find_education_laws internally calls search_fulltext with prefix filter + fallback)
Anchor demo query answerable in 1-2 tool calls
Tools are use-case driven: zhlaw_find_education_laws (412.x domain) vs zhlaw_search_laws (all laws)
ARCH-006.notes: 8 tools is appropriate for the dataset. No API-mapping anti-pattern detected.

evidence: Each tool returns atomically complete results: zhlaw_get_law returns full LawDetail including sr_number, title, abbreviation, urls, content
zhlaw_get_article returns ArticleItem with number, title, content, paragraphs, law, law_title, zhlex_url - complete without follow-up calls
zhlaw_find_education_laws does internal fallback to broader search, returning a complete result set
ARCH-007.notes: Tools deliver self-contained responses. No 'helper tools' that return only IDs requiring follow-up.

evidence: README.md explicitly documents Tools-only design decision under '### Design Decision: Tools-only (no MCP Resources)' with detailed rationale
Rationale: parametric lookups, 974 laws corpus too large for static Resources, MCP Resource templates deferred to Phase 2
ARCH-008.notes: Check passes because the README provides a documented rationale for tools-only approach.

evidence: All 8 tools have explicit annotations with all 4 fields: readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint, openWorldHint
7 read-only tools: readOnlyHint=True, destructiveHint=False, idempotentHint=True
zhlaw_get_law_metadata: openWorldHint=True (makes live HTTP request to zh.ch)
zhlaw_update_cache: readOnlyHint=False, openWorldHint=True (downloads from HuggingFace)
Annotations consistent with actual tool behavior
ARCH-009.notes: Full annotation coverage on all 8 tools. Values are semantically correct.

pyproject.toml with hatchling, [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages = ['src/openlex_mcp']

evidence: MCP_PROTOCOL_VERSION = '2025-11-25' constant in server.py line 64

evidence: responses.py: SOURCE constant = 'Kanton Zürich Rechtssammlung — HuggingFace rcds/swiss_legislation (CC-BY-SA 4.0) & zh.ch'
All response envelopes include source field with this attribution string
provenance field (Literal['cache','live','parser','cache+parser','none']) tracks data origin per response

evidence: _fail() function raises ToolError (isError result) for execution errors - not JSON-RPC protocol errors
All 8 tool handlers have try/except that routes to _fail() for unexpected exceptions
Not-found cases return normal results (count=0 + message) - never isError
test_execution_error_raises_masked_toolerror verifies ToolError raised on execution failure
test_not_found_is_a_normal_result_not_an_error verifies not-found is a normal result
OBS-001.notes: Clean separation: execution errors -> ToolError (isError), not-found -> normal result, schema errors -> rejected before tool runs.

evidence: _fail() logs original exception to stderr, then raises ToolError with api_client.handle_error() masked message
handle_error() explicitly masks unexpected errors: 'Ein unerwarteter interner Fehler ist aufgetreten.'
No traceback.format_exc() or sys.exc_info() in tool returns
No print() statements in src/ directory
test_execution_error_raises_masked_toolerror: verifies 'secret', 'RuntimeError', 'DB locked' do not appear in error
gaps: FastMCP() initialized without explicit mask_error_details=True parameter - masking achieved via manual ToolError wrapping pattern
OBS-002.notes: Effective masking via _fail() + handle_error() pattern. Tests confirm no internals leak.

evidence: structlog>=24.1.0 in pyproject.toml dependencies
logging_config.py: structlog configured with JSONRenderer, TimeStamper, add_log_level, format_exc_info
tool_logger() binds tool name + uuid correlation_id per call
All 8 tools use tlog = tool_logger('tool_name') with structured logging
test_execution_error_logs_structured_context: verifies tool, correlation_id, log_level, context fields
OBS-003.notes: Full structlog implementation with JSON output, bound per-tool context.

evidence: No print() statements found in src/ directory

structlog configured with PrintLoggerFactory(file=sys.stderr)

evidence: ROADMAP.md deferred-risk table explicitly documents: 'OBS-006 — OpenTelemetry tracing: Single-instance, single-tenant, read-only service. Distributed tracing adds no actionable signal until the service is multi-replica or multi-tenant. Scheduled for Phase 2.'

evidence: Settings.mcp_transport: Literal['stdio', 'streamable-http'] = 'stdio'
main(): use_http = settings.mcp_transport == 'streamable-http' or '--http' in sys.argv

evidence: README.md documents single-instance constraint: 'Single-instance only — horizontal scaling breaks active sessions because there is no shared session store'

evidence: CORS middleware exposes Mcp-Session-Id header (SDK-004 satisfied)

evidence: Dockerfile has 2 FROM statements: 'FROM python:3.11-slim AS builder' and 'FROM python:3.11-slim AS runtime'

evidence: compose.yml: deploy.resources.limits.memory=512m, cpus='0.5'

evidence: app_lifespan() decorated with @asynccontextmanager in server.py
FastMCP instantiated with lifespan=app_lifespan
Lifespan creates shared HTTP client via api_client.get_client() on entry
Lifespan closes client via await api_client.aclose_client() in finally block
test_lifespan_creates_and_closes_shared_client: verifies client created/closed by lifespan
SDK-001.notes: Proper FastMCP lifespan pattern. Single shared httpx.AsyncClient, no per-call creation.

evidence: responses.py: Envelope base class with source, provenance, count, message; 5 typed envelope subclasses

provenance Literal type for data origin tracking
source field with CC-BY-SA attribution string in all responses

evidence: zhlaw_update_cache has ctx: Context parameter

evidence: assert_url_allowed() rejects non-https schemes with EgressError
_is_blocked_ip() checks all resolved IPs against BLOCKED_NETWORKS
safe_get() runs full check on every redirect hop

evidence: net.py: _resolve() uses asyncio.getaddrinfo() to resolve host once
_pin_url() replaces hostname with resolved IP in URL
safe_get() pins connection to ips[0] with Host header and sni_hostname preserved

test_safe_get_blocks_redirect_to_unlisted_host: verifies redirect gate applies SSRF check
SEC-005.notes: DNS pinning properly implemented: one resolve, pin to IP, preserve Host/SNI for TLS validation.

evidence: Settings.mcp_transport default = 'stdio'

mcp.run() called without arguments in stdio mode (no network binding)

evidence: Multi-stage Dockerfile with non-root user uid 10001

python:3.11-slim runtime (minimal attack surface)

evidence: pyproject.toml: [project.scripts] openlex-mcp = 'openlex_mcp.server:main' - standard entry point, no custom cmdclass

evidence: README Safety section: 'Mcp-Session-Id generated and managed by the MCP SDK (cryptographically secure UUIDs)'

evidence: docs/secret-management.md: 'openlex-mcp holds no secrets. All data sources are public and require no credentials'

evidence: All 8 input models: model_config = ConfigDict(str_strip_whitespace=True, extra='forbid', strict=True)

test_search_input_strict_rejects_string_for_int_limit, test_search_input_strict_rejects_int_for_bool

evidence: README Safety: 'Security posture (Lethal Trifecta): Score 1/3: public data only · GET-only egress to www.zh.ch — no POST, no webhooks, no email · no code execution. Structurally safe by design.'
No private/sensitive data: only public Canton Zurich legislation
No external send capability: all tool handlers are read-only
No smtplib, no POST webhooks, no Slack integration
SEC-019.notes: Score 1/3 on Lethal Trifecta. Structurally safe for read-only public data server.

evidence: No os.system(), shell=True, eval(), exec(), or os.popen() found in src/ (grep confirmed clean)
No subprocess calls in server code
Database access via SQLite Python library, HTTP via httpx, dataset via datasets library

evidence: net.py: EGRESS_ALLOWLIST = frozenset({'www.zh.ch'}) - immutable, not runtime-mutable

assert_url_allowed() chains: HTTPS check -> host allowlist -> IP blocklist
docs/network-egress.md: documents code-layer and provides K8s NetworkPolicy example for network-layer
test_assert_host_allowed_passes_for_listed / test_assert_host_allowed_rejects_unlisted
gaps: Network-layer egress policy not implemented in current single-container deployment (documented as recommendation in docs/network-egress.md)

evidence: All 8 tool names use 'openlex__' namespace prefix preventing cross-server shadowing
